important_facts.content: The failover procedure requires draining connections for 60 seconds before switching the primary
After failover, the old primary must be manually re-added as a replica

The database team did a failover drill on March 1st
Monitoring alerts fire during planned failovers

query: What are the critical steps in the database failover procedure?
gold_answer: Drain connections for 60 seconds, switch primary, then manually re-add old primary as replica

important_facts.content: The token signing key is RSA 4096-bit stored in HSM, rotated quarterly
During key rotation, both old and new keys are valid for 24 hours

noise_facts.content: JWT library is jose4j
Token validation takes about 2ms
The auth service handles 5000 validations per second
OIDC discovery endpoint is at /.well-known/openid-configuration

gold_answer: RSA 4096-bit in HSM, rotated quarterly, both keys valid for 24h overlap

important_facts.content: The WAF blocks requests with SQL injection patterns and rate-limits IPs exceeding 1000 req/min globally
WAF bypass for internal health checks uses a shared secret in the X-Internal-Auth header

noise_facts.content: The WAF is AWS WAF v2
WAF rules were last updated in February
False positive rate is about 0.01%
The security team reviews WAF logs weekly
Geo-blocking is enabled for sanctioned countries

query: How do internal services bypass the WAF and what are the main WAF rules?
gold_answer: X-Internal-Auth header with shared secret; WAF blocks SQL injection and rate-limits at 1000 req/min per IP

important_facts.content: Zero-downtime migrations require: 1) backward-compatible schema change, 2) deploy new code, 3) backfill data, 4) deploy cleanup code

noise_facts.content: The last migration added an index on created_at
Migrations are tested against a production clone
The migration tool is Alembic
Average migration time is 30 seconds

query: What are the steps for a zero-downtime database migration?
gold_answer: 1) Backward-compatible schema change, 2) deploy new code, 3) backfill data, 4) deploy cleanup

important_facts.content: Multi-region failover order: us-east-1 (primary) → us-west-2 (warm standby) → eu-west-1 (cold standby)
Cold standby activation requires manual DNS update and takes 15-30 minutes

noise_facts.content: AWS us-east-1 has the most services

The DR test passed last month
Regional health is monitored by Route 53 health checks
The infrastructure team discussed adding ap-southeast-1

query: If both US regions go down, how do we recover and how long does it take?
gold_answer: Activate eu-west-1 cold standby via manual DNS update, takes 15-30 minutes

important_facts.content: The data classification levels are: Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted

query: What special handling does Restricted data require?

important_facts.content: The cost allocation tags required on all AWS resources: team, environment, service, cost-center

important_facts.content: API backward compatibility guarantee: deprecated endpoints maintained for 6 months with Sunset header
Breaking changes require RFC approval and 3-month migration support

noise_facts.content: We have 45 API endpoints
API docs are on readme.com

OpenAPI spec is auto-generated
API versioning uses URL path

query: What's our API deprecation policy?
gold_answer: Deprecated endpoints kept for 6 months with Sunset header; breaking changes need RFC approval and 3-month migration support

important_facts.content: The log level hierarchy for production: ERROR and WARN always logged, INFO sampled at 10%, DEBUG disabled
To enable DEBUG in production, set the X-Debug-Token header with a valid ops token

noise_facts.content: Logs are structured JSON
Log volume is about 50 GB per day
Loki is the log aggregation system
Log retention is 30 days
Request IDs are included in all log lines

query: How do you get DEBUG logs in production?
gold_answer: Set X-Debug-Token header with a valid ops token (DEBUG is normally disabled, INFO sampled at 10%)

important_facts.content: Database schema changes in production must go through the change advisory board (CAB) and require a rollback script
Emergency schema changes can bypass CAB with VP Engineering approval and post-incident review

noise_facts.content: CAB meets on Tuesdays at 2 PM
Schema changes are tracked in a changelog table
The last schema change added a UUID column
DBA review is recommended but not mandatory for index-only changes

query: How can an emergency schema change bypass normal approval?
gold_answer: VP Engineering approval plus mandatory post-incident review

important_facts.content: The service mesh mTLS certificate chain: root CA (offline, 10-year) → intermediate CA (Vault, 1-year) → service cert (auto-rotated every 24 hours)

noise_facts.content: Istio handles the service mesh
mTLS was enabled last quarter
Certificate errors caused a 5-minute outage in February
The security team audited the cert chain last month
SPIFFE IDs are used for service identity

query: Describe the mTLS certificate hierarchy and rotation schedule
gold_answer: Root CA offline with 10-year validity, intermediate CA in Vault with 1-year validity, service certs auto-rotate every 24 hours
